findings.title: listProjectIds() ignores DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT environment variable
findings.description: Line 223 uses `path.resolve("data")` directly instead of the `getDataRoot()` helper from `packages/core/src/paths.ts`, which respects the `DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT` env var (`process.env.DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT || "data"`). When `DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT` is set, project storage goes to the alternate location via `dataDir(id)`, but `listProjectIds()` still scans `./data`, returning either no projects or the wrong list. Running `pnpm deepsec export` without `--project-id` then produces empty/inconsistent output. The same bug exists in `metrics.ts:28`, `data-commit.ts:4`, and `sandbox-all.ts:23`.
findings.recommendation: Export a `getDataRoot()` accessor (or `dataRoot()` returning the resolved absolute path) from `packages/core/src/paths.ts` and use it everywhere instead of `path.resolve("data")`. Alternatively, factor a `listAllProjectIds()` helper into core that uses the same root resolution as `dataDir(id)`.

revalidation.reasoning: The current export.ts imports getDataRoot from @deepsec/core and listProjectIds() resolves path.resolve(getDataRoot()), not path.resolve('data'). getDataRoot() in packages/core/src/paths.ts returns process.env.DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT || 'data', which is the same root used by dataDir(projectId). The export path is internally consistent as well: projectRepoUrl reads path.join(dataDir(projectId), 'project.json'), and loadAllFileRecords also operates on the core data paths. I also checked the related locations mentioned in the finding: sandbox-all.ts discoverProjects(), metrics.ts discoverProjects(), and data-commit.ts now all use path.resolve(getDataRoot()). That means setting DEEPSEC_DATA_ROOT will affect project discovery and storage consistently in the current code. Git history was unavailable in this sandbox, but the present source has the recommended helper-based fix.
